thesis: The drone industry is racing to take the human out of the loop with drone-in-a-box autonomy. A Yup'ik village in Quinhagak is doing the opposite, and it works: they scaled the scarce input, trained local pilots who already read the land, and now they teach other villages. Autonomy fielded the tool; local knowledge is the decisive intelligence.
supporting_facts: Gleason (c10): Yup'ik and Alaska Native pilots excel 'because it's where they grew up.' The person running the training names local knowledge as the decisive variable, not the flight computer.

The org's behavior is the proof: drones first flew in for outsiders' work at the Nunalleq dig (c12); the village could have flown for hire, and instead built a 14-week course and now TEACHES Quinhagak, Eek, Goodnews Bay, Platinum (c13, c14). They scaled pilots, not boxes.

The industry contrast (2025 background, c16): the state SOAR program fields the US-made, autonomy-capable Skydio X10 and openly pursues Beyond Visual Line of Sight, drone-in-a-box operation over Starlink. The stated direction is to remove the operator; the win on the ground is a human-training win.
fair_counter_point_to_honor: The autonomy is genuinely real and genuinely fielded (22 drones and docks stationed in Alaska, 35 more planned, c17). That capability is exactly what lowered the barrier enough for a village to run its own program. This is not anti-AI: capable autonomy PLUS local expertise beats capable autonomy alone. Autonomy raised the ceiling; trained local pilots are what turn it into search-and-rescue and subsistence value.
valence: celebratory (a genuine, bounded Alaska-Native-led win), rotating off the recent curious/mixed streak.
honesty_guardrails: The KYUK reporting never says AI or autonomy; keep the human-knowledge frame primary as the source does.
AI/autonomy language attaches ONLY to the Skydio X10 platform and the SOAR program (2025 background, labeled), never to the villagers' words or actions.
Reindeer/salmon computer-vision counting is ASPIRATION ('wants to', 'aims to'), never a deployed system.
Do not claim AI finds the lost person; that is Petluska plus a thermal camera plus local knowledge (c09).

Cultural: name Nalaquq and the roles precisely; never name the village corporation (unverified); never present Yup'ik opinion as a bloc; pro-Alaska not savior; no characterized-object humor on any real named person; no Native iconography or unverified Native words on screen.
runner_up_angles: Straight self-determination / infrastructure-sovereignty (Analyst A): true and moving, but the more expected angle and quieter on AI. Grafted in as the spine, not the headline.
Pure SAR-utility 'drones save lives faster in the bush' (c08/c09): under-uses Gleason's c10 and the industry contrast, and risks sounding like a product testimonial. Used as one beat, not the thesis.
